title: Lasagne alla bolognese
sources: Pellegrino Artusi, La Scienza in Cucina e l'Arte di Mangiar Bene (1891) — canonical Italian source for ragù; recipe 87 (Maccheroni alla bolognese) gives the ragù model the modern lasagne draws on.

Accademia Italiana della Cucina official ragù alla bolognese recipe (1982, deposited at Bologna chamber of commerce) — for ingredient ratios; not a source to quote since it's modern, but a reference for orthodoxy.
Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), recipe for Italian macaroni dishes — period English-language context for an Anglophone audience.
notes: The deep-dive recipe in the batch — make the case for slow ragù, fresh egg pasta (or shop-bought), thin béchamel, six layers minimum. Pair with the existing béchamel anchor tutorial as a sub-tutorial card. Scalable. Freezes well. Make-ahead the ragù a day before. Use lasagne-sheets ingredient. Cite béchamel-anchor tutorial slug 'bechamel' for the sub-tutorial card.
